author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
On the words of the Gospel, Luke xxiv. 36, ‘He himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, peace be unto you,’ etc.

ccel.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

title: On the words of the Gospel, Luke xxiv. 36, ‘He himself stood in the midst of them, and saith unto them, peace be unto you,’ etc.

source.title: NPNF1-06. St. Augustine: Sermon on the Mount; Harmony of the Gospels; Homilies on the Gospels

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Lord appeared to His disciples after His resurrection, as ye have heard, and saluted them, saying, “Peace be unto you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is peace indeed, and the salutation of salvation: for the very word salutation has received its name from salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And what can be better than that Salvation Itself should salute man?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For Christ is our Salvation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He is our Salvation, who was wounded for us, and fixed by nails to the tree, and being taken down from the tree, was laid in the sepulchre.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And from the sepulchre He arose, with His wounds healed, His scars kept.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For this He judged expedient for His disciples, that His scars should be kept, where by the wounds of their hearts might be healed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What wounds?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The wounds of unbelief.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For He appeared to their eyes, exhibiting real flesh, and they thought they saw a spirit.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It is no light wound, this wound of the heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yea, they have made a malignant heresy who have abided in this wound.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But do we suppose that the disciples had not been wounded, because they were so quickly healed?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Only, Beloved, suppose, if they had continued in this wound, to think that the Body which had been buried, could not rise again, but that a spirit in the image of a body, deceived the eyes of men: if they had continued in this belief, yea, rather in this unbelief, not their wounds, but their death would have had to be bewailed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But what said the Lord Jesus?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Why are ye troubled, and why do thoughts ascend into your hearts?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thoughts ascend into your heart, the thoughts come from the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But it is good for a man, not that a thought should ascend into his heart, but that his heart should itself ascend upwards, where the Apostle would have believers place their hearts, to whom he said, “If ye be risen with Christ, mind those things which are above, where Christ is sitting at the right hand of God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Seek those things which are above, not the things which are upon the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For ye are dead, and your life is hid with Christ in God.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When Christ your life shall appear, then shall ye also appear with Him in glory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In what glory?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The glory of the resurrection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: In what glory?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear the Apostle saying of this body, “It is sown in dishonour, it shall rise in glory.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This glory the Apostles were unwilling to assign to their Master, their Christ, their Lord: they did not believe that His Body could rise from the sepulchre: they thought Him to be a Spirit, though they saw His flesh, and they believed not their very eyes.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet we believe them who preach but do not show Him.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, they believed not Christ who showed Himself to them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Malignant wound!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the remedies for these scars come forth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Why are ye troubled, and why do thoughts ascend into your hearts?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See My hands and My feet,” where I was fixed with the nails.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Handle and see.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But ye see, and yet do not see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Handle and see.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That a spirit hath not flesh and bones, as ye see me have.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When He had thus spoken,” so it is written, “He showed them His hands and His feet.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And while they were yet in hesitation, and wondered for joy.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now there was joy already, and yet hesitation continued.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For a thing incredible had taken place, yet taken place it had.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Is it at this day a thing incredible, that the Body of the Lord rose again from the sepulchre?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The whole cleansed world has believed it; whoso has not believed it, has remained in his uncleanness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet at that time it was incredible: and persuasion was addressed not to the eyes only, but to the hands also, that by the bodily senses faith might descend into their heart, and that faith so descending into their heart might be preached throughout the world to them who neither saw nor touched, and yet without doubting believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Have ye,” saith He, “anything to eat?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How much doeth the good Builder still to build up the edifice of faith?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He did not hunger, yet He asked to eat.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And He ate by an act of His power, not through necessity.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: So then let the disciples acknowledge the verity of His body, which the world has acknowledged at their preaching.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If haply there be any heretics who still in their hearts maintain that Christ exhibited Himself to sight, but that Christ’s was not very flesh; let them now lay aside that error, and let the Gospel persuade them.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We do but blame them for entertaining this conceit: He will damn them if they shall persevere in it.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who art thou who dost not believe that a body laid in the sepulchre could rise again?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: If thou art a Manichee, who dost not believe that He was crucified either, because thou dost not believe that He was even born, thou declarest that all that He showed was false.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He showed what was false, and dost thou speak the truth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost not lie with thy mouth, and did He lie in His body?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo thou dost suppose that He appeared unto the eyes of men what He really was not, that He was a spirit, not flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear Him: He loves thee, let Him not condemn thee.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Hear Him speaking: lo, He speaks to thee, thou unhappy one, He speaks to thee, “Why art thou troubled, and why do thoughts ascend into thine heart?”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “See,” saith He, “My hands and My feet.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Handle and see, because a spirit hath not flesh and bones as ye see Me have.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This spake the Truth, and did He deceive?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: It was a body then, it was flesh; that which had been buried, appeared.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let doubting perish, and meet praise ensue.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He showed himself then to the disciples.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is “Himself”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Head of His Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Church was foreseen by Him as in thee to be throughout the world, by the disciples it was not yet seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He showed the Head, He promised the Body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For what did He add next?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “These are the words which I spake to you, while I was yet with you.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is this, “While I was yet with you”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Was He not with them then when He was speaking to them?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “when I was yet with you”? was with you as mortal, which now I am not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I was with you when I had yet to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What is, “with you”?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: With you who were to die, Myself to die.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Now I am no more with you: for I am with those who are to die, Myself to die no more for ever.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This then is what I said to you.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That all things must be fulfilled which are written in the Law, and in the Prophets, and in the Psalms concerning Me.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I told you that all things must be fulfilled.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Then opened He their understanding.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Come then, O Lord, employ Thy keys, open, that we may understand.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, Thou dost tell all things, and yet are not believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou art thought to be a spirit, art touched, art rudely handled, and yet they who touch Thee hesitate.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost admonish them out of the Scriptures, and yet they understand Thee not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Their hearts are closed, open, and enter in.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Then opened He their understanding.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Open, O Lord, yea, open the heart of him who is in doubt concerning Christ.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Open “his” understanding who believes that Christ was a phantom.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Then opened He their understanding, that they might understand the Scriptures.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And “He said unto them.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That thus it behoved.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: That thus it is written, and thus it behoved.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “That Christ should suffer, and rise from the dead the third day.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And this they saw, they saw Him suffering, they saw Him hanging, they saw Him with them alive after His resurrection.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What then did they not see?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Body, that is, the Church.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Him they saw, her they saw not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They saw the Bridegroom, the Bride yet lay hid.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let him promise her too.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “Thus it is written, and thus it behoved Christ to suffer, and to rise from the dead the third day.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This is the Bridegroom, what of the Bride?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “And that repentance and remission of sins should be preached in His Name among all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: This the disciples did not yet see: they did not yet see the Church throughout all nations, beginning at Jerusalem.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They saw the Head, and they believed the Head touching the Body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By this which they saw, they believed that which they saw not.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: We too are like to them: we see something which they saw not, and something we do not see which they did see.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do we see, which they saw not?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Church throughout all nations.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What do we not see, which they saw?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ present in the flesh.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: As they saw Him, and believed concerning the Body, so do we see the Body; let us believe concerning the Head.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let what we have respectively seen help us.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The sight of Christ helped them to believe the future Church: the sight of the Church helps us to believe that Christ has risen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Their faith was made complete, and ours is made complete also.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Their faith was made complete from the sight of the Head, ours is made complete by the sight of the Body.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Christ was made known to them “wholly,” and to us is He so made known: but He was not seen “wholly” by them, nor by us has He been “wholly” seen.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By them the Head was seen, the Body believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By us the Body has been seen, the Head believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet to none is Christ lacking: in all He is complete, though to this day His Body remains imperfect.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The Apostles believed; through them many of the inhabitants of Jerusalem believed; Judæa believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Samaria believed.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the members be added on, the building added on to the foundation.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “For no other foundation can any man lay,” says the Apostle, “than that which is laid, which is Christ Jesus.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the Jews rage madly, and be filled with jealousy: Stephen be stoned, Saul keep the raiment of them who stone him, Saul, one day to be the Apostle Paul.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Stephen be killed, the Church of Jerusalem dispersed in confusion: out of it go forth burning brands, and spread themselves and spread their flame.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For in the Church of Jerusalem, as it were burning brands were set on fire by the Holy Spirit, when they had all one soul, and one heart to God-ward.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When Stephen was stoned, that pile suffered persecution: the brands were dispersed, and the world was set on fire.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And then intent on his furious schemes, that Saul received letters from the chief of the priests, and began his journey in his cruel rage, breathing out slaughter, thirsting for blood, to drag bound and to hurry off to punishment whomsoever he could, and from every quarter that he could, and to satiate himself with the shedding of their blood.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But where was God, where was Christ, where He that had crowned Stephen?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Where, but in heaven?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let Him now look on Saul, and mock him in his fury, and call from heaven, “‘Saul, Saul, why persecutest thou Me?’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: I am in heaven, and thou in earth, and yet thou persecutest Me.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Thou dost not touch the body, but my members thou art treading down.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Yet what art thou doing?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: What art thou gaining?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: ‘It is hard for thee to kick against the pricks.’

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Kick as thou wilt, thou only distressest thyself.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lay aside thy fury then, recover soundness.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lay aside evil counsel, seek after good succour.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: By that voice he was struck to the earth.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Who was struck to the earth?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: The persecutor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Lo, by that one word was he overcome.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: After what wast thou going, after what was thy fury carrying thee?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Those whom thou wast seeking out, now thou followest; whom thou wast persecuting, now for them thou sufferest persecution.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He rises up the preacher, who was struck to the earth, the persecutor.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He heard the Lord’s voice.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was blinded, but in the body only, that he might be enlightened in heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: He was brought to Ananias, catechised on sundry points, baptized, and so came forth an Apostle.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Speak then, preach, preach Christ, spread His doctrine, O thou goodly leader of the flock, but lately a wolf.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: See him, mark him, who once was raging.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: “But for me, God forbid that I should glory, save in the Cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world has been crucified to me and I to the world.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Spread the Gospel: scatter with thy mouth what thou hast conceived in thine heart.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Let the nations hear, let the nations believe; let the nations multiply, let the Lord’s empurpled spouse spring forth from the blood of Martyrs.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: And from her how many have come already, how many members have cleaved to the Head, and cleave to Him still and believe!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: They were baptized, and others shall be baptized, and after them shall others come.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Then I say, at the end of the world shall the stones be joined to the foundation, living stones, holy stones, that at the end the whole edifice may be built by that Church, yea by this very Church which now sings the new song, while the house is in building.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: For so the Psalm itself says, “When the house was in building after the captivity;” and what says it, “Sing unto the Lord a new song, sing unto the Lord all the earth.”

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: How great a house is this!

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: But when does it sing the new song?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When it is in building.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When is it dedicated?

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: At the end of the world.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: Its foundation has been already dedicated, because He hath ascended into heaven, and dieth no more.

verses.book: Sermons on Selected Lessons of the New Testament.
verses.text: When we too shall have risen to die no more, then shall we be dedicated.
